nodes.name: agent.ts
nodes.summary: CLI command for managing custom agents (create, list, edit, delete) with support for defining system prompts, allowed tools, and agent modes (primary/subagent).

nodes.name: auth.ts
nodes.summary: CLI commands for authentication management including login, logout, and listing configured provider credentials with plugin-based provider resolution.

nodes.name: cmd.ts
nodes.summary: Tiny type-safe wrapper around yargs CommandModule that adds double-dash argument support, used by all other CLI command definitions.

nodes.name: db.ts
nodes.summary: CLI commands for database management including migrations, JSON-to-SQLite migration, and clearing session data from the local storage database.

nodes.name: agent.ts
nodes.summary: Debug CLI command for testing agent tool execution in isolation, allowing developers to invoke individual tools with specific parameters outside a full session.

nodes.name: config.ts
nodes.summary: Debug CLI command that dumps the resolved configuration for inspection and troubleshooting.

nodes.name: file.ts
nodes.summary: Debug CLI command for testing file operations and the file system abstraction layer, useful for diagnosing file reading and indexing issues.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel entry point for the debug CLI subcommand group, registering all debug subcommands (agent, config, file, lsp, ripgrep, scrap, skill, snapshot).

nodes.name: lsp.ts
nodes.summary: Debug CLI commands for testing Language Server Protocol integration, including workspace symbols and document symbols queries.

nodes.name: ripgrep.ts
nodes.summary: Debug CLI command for testing ripgrep search integration, allowing direct invocation of the underlying grep tool with various options.

nodes.name: scrap.ts
nodes.summary: Debug scratch pad command for ad-hoc development experiments and temporary code testing during development.

nodes.name: skill.ts
nodes.summary: Debug CLI command for testing skill resolution and execution in isolation.

nodes.name: snapshot.ts
nodes.summary: Debug CLI command for testing the snapshot system, which captures and restores project file states.

nodes.name: export.ts
nodes.summary: CLI command to export session conversation data to markdown or JSON format for sharing or archival purposes.

nodes.name: generate.ts
nodes.summary: CLI command to generate OpenAPI specification from the server routes for SDK and documentation generation.

nodes.name: github.ts
nodes.summary: Comprehensive GitHub Actions integration providing automated issue and PR handling, OIDC authentication, branch management, code review workflows, and CI/CD bot functionality.
nodes.languageNotes: Heavy use of GitHub REST and GraphQL APIs via Octokit, with complex workflow dispatch event handling for multiple trigger types (issues, PRs, review comments, workflow dispatch).

nodes.name: import.ts
nodes.summary: CLI command to import shared session conversations from URLs or JSON files, transforming share data into local session records.

nodes.name: mcp.ts
nodes.summary: CLI commands for managing MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers including listing, adding, authenticating, debugging, and configuring local and remote MCP server connections.

nodes.name: models.ts
nodes.summary: CLI command to list available AI models from configured providers, with optional verbose output showing model capabilities and pricing.

nodes.name: pr.ts
nodes.summary: CLI command providing a guided pull request creation workflow with interactive prompts for title, description, and review options.

nodes.name: run.ts
nodes.summary: Non-interactive headless run command that executes an AI agent session with streaming output, tool result rendering, and SDK client integration for programmatic usage.
nodes.languageNotes: Registers all available tools (glob, grep, read, write, edit, bash, etc.) and maps tool execution results to formatted terminal output with inline/block rendering modes.

nodes.name: serve.ts
nodes.summary: CLI command to start the local HTTP server that powers the SDK API and TUI communication layer.

nodes.name: session.ts
nodes.summary: CLI commands for session lifecycle management including listing sessions with table/JSON output formatting and deleting individual sessions.

nodes.name: stats.ts
nodes.summary: CLI command to display usage statistics including token consumption, cost breakdown by model, tool usage counts, and session aggregation over configurable time windows.

nodes.name: app.tsx
nodes.summary: Root TUI application component built with SolidJS and OpenTUI, providing the interactive terminal interface with keyboard shortcuts, theme detection, dialog management, and session routing.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses SolidJS reactive primitives with a custom terminal rendering engine (OpenTUI), wrapping the entire app in nested context providers for SDK, sync, theming, keybinds, and prompt state.

nodes.name: getUserPrompt
nodes.summary: Assembles the user prompt for GitHub-triggered agent sessions by combining issue/PR context, file references, and user instructions into a structured message.

nodes.name: chat
nodes.summary: Executes a full agent chat session within a GitHub Actions context, managing session creation, message streaming, event subscription, and response summarization.

nodes.name: fetchPR
nodes.summary: Fetches comprehensive PR data from GitHub including diff, review comments, and requested changes using both REST and GraphQL APIs.

nodes.name: createPR
nodes.summary: Creates a new GitHub pull request with auto-generated title and body, handling fork-based and direct branch workflows.

nodes.name: subscribeSessionEvents
nodes.summary: Sets up event listeners for agent session events (tool calls, text output, errors) and formats them for GitHub Actions log output.

nodes.name: installGitHubApp
nodes.summary: Guides the user through GitHub App installation with interactive prompts, generating and configuring workflow files for the repository.

nodes.name: addWorkflowFiles
nodes.summary: Writes GitHub Actions workflow YAML files to the repository for automated issue and PR handling.

nodes.name: execute
nodes.summary: Main execution loop for the headless run command that processes agent messages, renders tool outputs, handles permission prompts, and manages the session lifecycle.

nodes.name: aggregateSessionStats
nodes.summary: Aggregates usage statistics across all sessions within a time window, computing token totals, cost breakdowns by model, tool usage frequencies, and per-session medians.

nodes.name: displayStats
nodes.summary: Renders aggregated session statistics to the terminal with formatted tables showing costs, token usage, tool invocations, and model breakdowns.

nodes.name: App
nodes.summary: Root SolidJS component for the TUI that manages global keyboard shortcuts, dialog layers, theme switching, session navigation, and provider context composition.

nodes.name: getTerminalBackgroundColor
nodes.summary: Detects the terminal background color (dark or light) by sending ANSI escape sequences and parsing the terminal's response for theme auto-detection.

nodes.name: resolveConfigPath
nodes.summary: Resolves the appropriate MCP configuration file path based on whether global or project-local scope is requested.

nodes.name: addMcpToConfig
nodes.summary: Adds or updates an MCP server configuration entry in the JSONC config file using non-destructive edits that preserve comments.

nodes.name: createToolContext
nodes.summary: Constructs a mock tool execution context for debug purposes, allowing individual tools to be tested outside a real agent session.

nodes.name: parseToolParams
nodes.summary: Parses tool parameters from command-line key=value pairs or JSON input into the structured format expected by tool handlers.

nodes.name: SessionStats
nodes.summary: Interface defining the shape of aggregated session statistics including token totals, cost breakdowns, tool usage, and per-model metrics.

nodes.name: formatSessionTable
nodes.summary: Formats a list of sessions into a columnar table with ID, title, date, message count, and cost for terminal display.

nodes.name: pagerCmd
nodes.summary: Detects and returns the appropriate system pager command (less, more, or cat) for paginating long session output.

nodes.name: printModels
nodes.summary: Fetches and prints available models for a given provider, formatting them as a list with optional verbose details.
